report_text: NOTE: This CSV contains entirely simulated radiology reports created for demonstration purposes. These are not real patient reports. No real patient data is included in this repository. The reports are modelled on the structure and language of PET-CT oncology reports to provide realistic test data for the extraction pipeline.

PET FDG Whole Body :
Skull base to upper thigh.
Blood glucose=5.4 mmol/l.
No relevant prior study available for comparison.

Findings:
1. There is moderately FDG-avid (SUV max = 8.7) concentric thickening of the sigmoid colon extending for approximately 4 cm.
2. There is a 1.2 cm focus of markedly increased FDG uptake (SUVmax=14.1) in segment VII of the liver, suspicious for a solitary hepatic metastasis.
3. A further sub-centimetre low-density lesion in segment III is not significantly FDG-avid and is too small to characterise with PET.
4. There are two mildly FDG-avid (SUVmax=3.2) pericolic lymph nodes adjacent to the primary tumour.
5. A low-density 2.1 cm nodule arising from the left adrenal gland is background FDG-avid, in keeping with an incidental adenoma.
6. The remainder of FDG uptake is physiological. The unenhanced CT does not add further clinically relevant information.

Conclusion:
Moderately FDG-avid sigmoid colonic tumour with a solitary markedly FDG-avid hepatic metastasis in segment VII. Pericolic lymphadenopathy. Left adrenal adenoma.

PET/CT 18FDG Whole Body :

Findings:
Skull vertex to upper thighs.
Blood glucose: 5.8 mmol/litre. Non-diabetic.
Comparison: PET/CT 03/06/2024.

1. Interval right mastectomy and axillary clearance. There is moderate FDG uptake in the surgical bed consistent with post-operative change (SUVmax=4.2).
2. Increase in size and FDG avidity of bilateral internal mammary and mediastinal lymphadenopathy, e.g. prevascular node now 1.9 cm (previously 1.1 cm, SUVmax=11.8, previously 5.4). New mildly FDG-avid right supraclavicular node (SUVmax=3.6).
3. New small moderately FDG-avid hepatic foci in segments II and IVa (SUVmax=7.1 and 5.9 respectively), suspicious for metastatic disease. Previously unremarkable liver.
4. Marked progression of bone metastases with new FDG-avid foci in T7 vertebral body (SUVmax=9.3), right iliac wing (SUVmax=6.8), and left femoral neck (SUVmax=5.1). Previously identified L3 lesion has increased in avidity (SUVmax=12.4, previously 7.6).
5. New FDG-avid 8mm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule (SUVmax=4.7). Stable 5mm left lower lobe nodule remains not FDG-avid.
6. Foci of reduced uptake in the left parietal lobe with corresponding low attenuation on CT, concerning for intracranial metastatic disease. No midline shift.
7. The remainder of FDG distribution is physiological.

Comment:
Marked disease progression with nodal, hepatic, osseous, pulmonary and possible intracranial metastases. MRI brain recommended for further evaluation of the left parietal lesion. The structural bone lesion at left femoral neck should be assessed for fracture risk.
ICD10-Z087

Technique: Skull base to upper thighs, 362.7 MBq of FDG injected, blood glucose 5.9 mmol per litre.

Findings: Compared to the PET/CT scan of 14/08/2024.
#1. There is normal physiological distribution of FDG.
#2. No FDG-avid lymphadenopathy above or below the diaphragm.
#3. The CT component of the scan does not demonstrate any new clinically significant abnormality.

Conclusion: No evidence of local or distant disease relapse.

ICD 10 - Z080
